rules: Price is an external validation outcome ONLY; it never enters construction, normalization, weighting, or selection.
Every candidate and weight is pre-registered in backend/desirability/collector_appeal.py.
Desirability is applied exactly once, in D.
Fixed normalization only; no cohort percentiles in any shippable construct.
Canonical RIP is unchanged. Nothing is wired into production. No database writes occur.

exclusions.reason: no hit-eligible card with both an appeal link and a modeled pull rate

degeneracy_notes.CA0_desirability_only: D. Carries no structural information by construction; the simplicity benchmark.
degeneracy_notes.CA1_accessible: D*A - monotone INCREASING in A. A pure accessibility taste.
degeneracy_notes.CA2_chase: D*(1-A) - monotone DECREASING in A. A pure scarcity taste. This is D x M*.
degeneracy_notes.CA3_geometric_balance: D*sqrt(A*(1-A)) - a HUMP peaking at A=0.50. Rewards the middle BY CONSTRUCTION, not by evidence. NOT INJECTIVE: A=0.2 and A=0.8 score identically, so a highly accessible set and an extreme-chase set are declared equally appealing.
degeneracy_notes.CA4_linear_75_25: D*(+0.50*A + 0.25) - linear in A, favouring accessibility. Structural content scales with |wA-wM| = 0.50.
degeneracy_notes.CA4_linear_60_40: D*(+0.20*A + 0.40) - linear in A, favouring accessibility. Structural content scales with |wA-wM| = 0.20.
degeneracy_notes.CA4_linear_50_50: D*((wA-wM)*A + wM) with wA=wM -> the A term CANCELS and the candidate collapses to a rescaled D (0.50*D). Zero structural content: fully DEGENERATE.
degeneracy_notes.CA4_linear_40_60: D*(-0.20*A + 0.60) - linear in A, favouring chase intensity. Structural content scales with |wA-wM| = 0.20.
degeneracy_notes.CA4_linear_25_75: D*(-0.50*A + 0.75) - linear in A, favouring chase intensity. Structural content scales with |wA-wM| = 0.50.
degeneracy_notes.CA5_interaction_45_45_10: D*(cancels (wA=wM) + 0.45 + 0.10*A*(1-A)) - the interaction term A*(1-A) is the SAME hump as CA3. With wA=wM the linear part cancels and the candidate becomes a pure rescaled hump: it rewards the middle by construction.
degeneracy_notes.CA5_interaction_40_40_20: D*(cancels (wA=wM) + 0.40 + 0.20*A*(1-A)) - the interaction term A*(1-A) is the SAME hump as CA3. With wA=wM the linear part cancels and the candidate becomes a pure rescaled hump: it rewards the middle by construction.
degeneracy_notes.CA5_interaction_35_35_30: D*(cancels (wA=wM) + 0.35 + 0.30*A*(1-A)) - the interaction term A*(1-A) is the SAME hump as CA3. With wA=wM the linear part cancels and the candidate becomes a pure rescaled hump: it rewards the middle by construction.
degeneracy_notes.CA6_dual_path_utility: D*(floor + gain*P). Independent of position on the A<->M axis by construction; reads the ORTHOGONAL dual-path structure instead. Does not encode a taste.

CA0_desirability_only.degeneracy_note: D. Carries no structural information by construction; the simplicity benchmark.

CA2_chase.degeneracy_note: D*(1-A) - monotone DECREASING in A. A pure scarcity taste. This is D x M*.

CA3_geometric_balance.degeneracy_note: D*sqrt(A*(1-A)) - a HUMP peaking at A=0.50. Rewards the middle BY CONSTRUCTION, not by evidence. NOT INJECTIVE: A=0.2 and A=0.8 score identically, so a highly accessible set and an extreme-chase set are declared equally appealing.

CA4_linear_75_25.degeneracy_note: D*(+0.50*A + 0.25) - linear in A, favouring accessibility. Structural content scales with |wA-wM| = 0.50.

CA4_linear_60_40.degeneracy_note: D*(+0.20*A + 0.40) - linear in A, favouring accessibility. Structural content scales with |wA-wM| = 0.20.

CA4_linear_50_50.degeneracy_note: D*((wA-wM)*A + wM) with wA=wM -> the A term CANCELS and the candidate collapses to a rescaled D (0.50*D). Zero structural content: fully DEGENERATE.

CA4_linear_40_60.degeneracy_note: D*(-0.20*A + 0.60) - linear in A, favouring chase intensity. Structural content scales with |wA-wM| = 0.20.

CA4_linear_25_75.degeneracy_note: D*(-0.50*A + 0.75) - linear in A, favouring chase intensity. Structural content scales with |wA-wM| = 0.50.

CA5_interaction_45_45_10.degeneracy_note: D*(cancels (wA=wM) + 0.45 + 0.10*A*(1-A)) - the interaction term A*(1-A) is the SAME hump as CA3. With wA=wM the linear part cancels and the candidate becomes a pure rescaled hump: it rewards the middle by construction.

CA5_interaction_40_40_20.degeneracy_note: D*(cancels (wA=wM) + 0.40 + 0.20*A*(1-A)) - the interaction term A*(1-A) is the SAME hump as CA3. With wA=wM the linear part cancels and the candidate becomes a pure rescaled hump: it rewards the middle by construction.

CA5_interaction_35_35_30.degeneracy_note: D*(cancels (wA=wM) + 0.35 + 0.30*A*(1-A)) - the interaction term A*(1-A) is the SAME hump as CA3. With wA=wM the linear part cancels and the candidate becomes a pure rescaled hump: it rewards the middle by construction.

CA6_dual_path_utility.degeneracy_note: D*(floor + gain*P). Independent of position on the A<->M axis by construction; reads the ORTHOGONAL dual-path structure instead. Does not encode a taste.

market_relationships._note: Value-LEVEL and value-CONCENTRATION outcomes are reported separately. A stronger relationship with concentration is not automatically better; it answers a different question (how value is distributed, not how much there is).

size_bias.question: Does Chase Appeal explain market value beyond the number of chances a large set has to contain desirable rare cards?

excess_chase_intensity.note: Expectation fitted on size controls only; price is never a regressor.

excess_chase_appeal.note: Expectation fitted on size controls only; price is never a regressor.

rip_weight_analysis.primary_method: proportional rescaling of the 58:20:12 financial ratio
rip_weight_analysis.second_largest_crossover_note: Under proportional rescaling Safety shrinks as Collector Appeal grows, so they cross at w = (20/90)/(1 + 20/90) = 2/11 = 18.18%, NOT at 25% as the brief assumed. Collector Appeal is already the second-largest pillar at a 20% weight.
rip_weight_analysis.note: Research-only variants. Canonical RIP is unchanged.

desirability_uncertainty._note: Desirability carries no measured uncertainty (it is a single snapshot with no repeated observations), so these are assumption scenarios, not estimated bounds.

pull_rate_uncertainty._note: Uncertainty SCENARIOS, not empirically estimated confidence bounds: the modeled pull rates are config-derived and carry no source sample counts to propagate.
